# Patient Path NYC

> Independent patient advocacy practice in New York City, run by Shlomit Liberty (MS, MA, CCC-SLP, BCPA) — a Board Certified Patient Advocate and medical Speech-Language Pathologist with more than a decade of clinical experience at NYC and Stanford hospitals. The practice provides personal medical crisis management, care coordination, and safe discharge planning for hospitalized adults and their families.

The practice works only with inpatients — adults currently admitted to acute care hospitals, acute rehabilitation centers, or subacute rehabilitation facilities. Services include a free 20-minute initial consultation, in-hospital care management (daily chart review, team communication, bedside visits, family meeting facilitation), and discharge planning with ongoing involvement through the transition home or to another facility. The practice is private-pay only; it does not accept insurance, which keeps its loyalty exclusively with the patient and family.

Service area: New York City (Manhattan, Brooklyn, Queens, the Bronx), Nassau County, Westchester County, and South Fairfield County, CT.

## Main pages

- [Home](https://www.patientpathnyc.com/): Overview of the practice, services, trust strip of hospitals and professional affiliations, testimonials, conditions list, and contact form.
- [About Shlomit](https://www.patientpathnyc.com/about.html): Founder bio, credentials, education, awards, professional affiliations.
- [FAQ](https://www.patientpathnyc.com/faq.html): First-person answers to common questions about who the practice works with, how it differs from a hospital social worker, insurance, and how to get started.
- [Resources](https://www.patientpathnyc.com/resources.html): Editorial index of all guides.

## Resource guides

- [Navigating Your Hospital Stay](https://www.patientpathnyc.com/navigating-hospital-stay.html): Preparation, communication, and infection prevention from admission to discharge.
- [The Medical History You Should Bring](https://www.patientpathnyc.com/medical-history-form.html): What to gather (medications, specialists, allergies, surgeries, advance directives) and why each item matters.
- [How to Pack for a Hospital Stay](https://www.patientpathnyc.com/pack-for-hospital-stay.html): Medical-info and personal-items checklists for a ready-to-go hospital bag.
- [How to Communicate Your Symptoms](https://www.patientpathnyc.com/communicate-symptoms.html): The STARS framework — Specific location, Treatment tried, Associated symptoms, Relief factors, Severity level.
- [How to Fight Hospital-Acquired Infections](https://www.patientpathnyc.com/fight-hospital-infections.html): Five evidence-based prevention practices — oral care, masks, hand hygiene, surface disinfection, reporting early warning signs.
- [How to Prepare for a Safe Discharge](https://www.patientpathnyc.com/safe-discharge.html): Levels of care after a hospital stay (acute rehab, sub-acute, LTACH, home health, outpatient) and the CUS Tool (Concerned, Uncomfortable, Safety) for self-advocacy.
- [How to Prevent Cognitive Decline During Hospitalization](https://www.patientpathnyc.com/prevent-cognitive-decline.html): Four practices to protect memory, attention, and clarity — orientation, cognitive engagement, assistive devices, movement.

## Founder

Shlomit Liberty, MS, MA, CCC-SLP, BCPA — Board Certified Patient Advocate and medical Speech-Language Pathologist. Clinical experience at New York Presbyterian Weill Cornell, Stanford University Hospital, and San Jose Regional Medical Center. Education: MS Communicative Sciences and Disorders (NYU), MA Linguistics (Hebrew University), BA Cognitive Science and Linguistics (Hebrew University). Co-creator of the FEES Foundations Course at Dysphagia Evaluation Specialists. ASHA Award for Continuing Education 2019–2023; Best Relief Staff, Stanford Rehab 2021.
